items.text: Email received — Rachel Thornton, DLA Piper LLP
PA identity & programme verified — DLA-UK-CC-0089 confirmed (CRM)

3 distinct requests identified — handling in parallel
  → R1: Programme limit reallocation, 47 cards, UK Marketing sub-programme
  → R2: New cardholder onboarding, 8 joiners, Consulting sub-programme
  → R3: Virtual card payment query, SUP-0441, PO #PO-2026-0298

Voice note received — WhatsApp, 0:43
  └─ Sender matched — linked to case CSC-2026-0322-DLA-0193
Language identified: Spanish — transcription complete
Content confirmed — voice note aligns with email requests, no new information
── REQUEST 1: Programme Limit Reallocation (47 cards) ──────────────
Programme credit line validated — headroom confirmed (MiVision)
Card-level eligibility sweep initiated — 47 cards across Auth Platform + SAS

Sweep complete — 46 clean, 1 flagged
  └─ James Osei ••••3847 — open SAS alert SAL-2026-0228-3847 (11 days, unresolved)
Inlogik CMP — bulk limit amendment executing for 46 clean cards
✓ 46 cards updated £3,000 → £5,000
⏸ AWAITING SM DECISION — card ••••3847 held

options.label: Option A
options.description: Hold limit increase for ••••3847. Notify PA that 46/47 cards updated and one is under review. No further escalation.
options.riskNote: SAS alert continues to sit unactioned.

options.label: Option B — Recommended
options.description: Hold limit increase. Escalate SAS alert SAL-2026-0228-3847 to Fraud Ops for urgent disposition. Send PA a holding response confirming 46/47 complete, one card under active review.
options.riskNote: Appropriate escalation. Alert actioned. PA informed.

options.label: Option C
options.description: Process limit increase for all 47 including ••••3847.
options.riskNote: Increases exposure on a card with an unresolved fraud flag. Compliance risk.

items.text: ── REQUEST 2: New Cardholder Onboarding (8 cards) ──────────────────
CRM hierarchy check — Consulting sub-programme confirmed
Google Sheet attachment detected — language identified: Spanish
Document extraction complete — 8 cardholder records extracted and structured in English
Inlogik CMP — issuing 8 cards at £4,000 standard limit
✓ 8 cards issued and added to programme hierarchy
MiVision — confirmed cards visible to PA
✓ Draft email queued — card details for 8 new joiners
── REQUEST 3: SAP Taulia Virtual Card Query ─────────────────────────
Supplier query received — SUP-0441, PO #PO-2026-0298
SAP Taulia portal — locating virtual card against PO
Virtual card found — VC-2026-0298-HSBC, issued 2026-03-14
Auth Platform — checking authorisation status
✓ Payment authorised and settled — GBP 18,400 on 2026-03-15
✓ Draft email queued — settlement reference STL-2026-0315-VC0298 provided to PA
── REQUEST 1 RESUMED — SM selected Option B ────────────────────────
SAS alert SAL-2026-0228-3847 escalated to Fraud Ops
✓ Holding response drafted — PA notified: 46/47 complete, 1 under review
CRM case logged — CSC-2026-0322-DLA-0193
✓ Full client response queued for SM approval

data.subject: Re: Programme DLA-UK-CC-0089 — Three Service Requests
data.body:
Dear Rachel,

Thank you for your email regarding programme DLA-UK-CC-0089. I'm pleased to confirm the following updates on your three requests:

Request 1 — Programme Limit Reallocation (UK Marketing):
We have successfully increased individual spend limits from £3,000 to £5,000 for 46 of the 47 cards in your UK Marketing sub-programme, effective immediately.

One card (ending ••••3847) has been temporarily held as it is subject to an active internal review. Our team is working to resolve this as a priority.

Request 2 — New Cardholder Onboarding (Consulting):
All 8 new corporate cards for your Consulting sub-programme have been successfully issued at the standard £4,000 limit. The cards are now active and visible to you in MiVision.

Request 3 — Virtual Card Payment Query (SUP-0441, PO #PO-2026-0298):
Payment of GBP 18,400 was authorised and settled on 15 March 2026. Settlement reference: STL-2026-0315-VC0298.

Kind regards,
[SM Name]
HSBC Commercial Cards
